title: Add prediction mode for B-Tree: 'which child will this key go to?'
description: The single most impactful practice feature per learning science (Roediger & Karpicke 2006: active recall retains 80% vs 34% passive). Before the B-Tree algorithm shows the next step, pause and ask: 'Key 35 is being searched. Root has [20, 40]. Which child?' User clicks a node. System reveals whether correct. Track score. Implementation: add predictionMode boolean to useDatabaseModule, pause step advancement, render clickable node targets in BTreeCanvas, compare click against next step's highlightNodeId.
acceptanceCriteria: Prediction Mode toggle in B-Tree sidebar (off by default)
When enabled, search pauses before each traversal step with prompt: 'Click the node key 35 will go to'
Correct: green flash + 'Correct! 35 > 20 and 35 < 40, so middle child.' explanation
Wrong: red flash + 'Not quite. 35 > 20, so not the left child.' + show correct path
Score counter: X/Y correct predictions this session
Works for both search and insert operations

title: Add prediction mode for Hash Index: 'which bucket will this key hash to?'
description: Before hash insert shows the bucket, ask: 'Key alice, hash=2741, 4 buckets. Which bucket?' User clicks a bucket or enters a number. Feedback explains the modulo arithmetic. Also: 'Bucket 2 has 3 entries (max 3). What happens?' → overflow chain / resize. Source: testing effect research — active computation > passive watching.
acceptanceCriteria: Prediction Mode toggle in Hash Index sidebar
Hash computation prediction: user calculates bucket number from hash value
Collision prediction: 'Is this bucket full? What happens next?'
Correct/wrong feedback with explanation of the modulo arithmetic
Score counter per session

title: Add prediction mode for Transaction Isolation: 'what will this read return?'
description: Before revealing the anomaly step, ask: 'T1 reads balance. T2 wrote 500 but hasn't committed. At READ UNCOMMITTED, what does T1 see?' User chooses: 500 (uncommitted) or 1000 (committed). Feedback: 'Correct! At READ UNCOMMITTED, T1 sees the uncommitted value. This is a dirty read.' Works for all 4 isolation levels × their anomaly steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Prediction prompt appears BEFORE each anomaly step in all 4 isolation levels
For dirty read: 'What value will T2 read?' → 500 (dirty) or 1000 (committed)
For non-repeatable: 'Will T1's second read match the first?' → Yes/No
For phantom: 'How many rows will T1's second COUNT return?' → 3 or 4
For serializable: 'Will T2's UPDATE succeed immediately?' → Yes/No (blocked)
Correct/wrong feedback explains the isolation level's behavior

title: Add manual normalization tracing: drag columns to decompose
description: Instead of automatic decomposeTo3NF, let the student manually decompose by DRAGGING columns between table cards. System validates in real-time: 'This decomposition is lossless? Yes/No. Dependency-preserving? Yes/No. Normal form of each table: [NF].' Uses existing computeClosure and determineNormalForm for validation. Transforms Bloom's Apply → Create.
acceptanceCriteria: Manual Decompose toggle in Normalization sidebar
Drag-and-drop column pills between table cards
Real-time validation: lossless (green/red), dependency-preserving (green/red), NF per table
If user creates a valid 3NF decomposition: success celebration + comparison with automatic result
If stuck: 'Show Hint' button reveals one FD violation to fix

title: Add LSM-Tree read path prediction: 'where does the search go next?'
description: During LSM read, after each level check: 'Key x not in memtable. Where does the search go next?' User picks: immutable memtable / L0 newest SSTable / L1 / not found. Teaches the top-to-bottom search priority that defines LSM read amplification.
acceptanceCriteria: Prediction prompt after each 'not found' step during LSM read
Options match the actual next search location from LSMTreeViz.read()
Correct: 'Right! LSM reads check memtable first, then L0 (newest to oldest), then L1, L2.'
Wrong: 'Not quite. After memtable, we check the IMMUTABLE memtable (if it exists) before L0.'
Score tracked per read operation

title: Add comparison challenges: B-Tree vs Hash, LSM vs B-Tree, SQL vs NoSQL
description: 5 scenario-based comparison questions testing Bloom's Analyze level: (1) 'Range query needed. B-Tree or Hash?' (2) '95% writes. LSM or B-Tree?' (3) 'Banking app consistency. Strong or Eventual?' (4) 'Date range sharding. Hash or Range?' (5) '3NF vs BCNF: need dependency preservation.' Each shows a scenario, user picks, system explains WHY the answer is correct using the module's existing visualizations as evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Comparison Challenges section accessible from sidebar or bottom panel
At least 5 scenario questions with multiple-choice answers
Each question has context scenario, 2-3 options, and detailed explanation
Explanation LINKS to the relevant visualization: 'See B-Tree range scan in the B-Tree mode'
Score tracked separately: 'Comparison Skills: 4/5'

title: Add debugging challenges: find the bug in B-Tree/Hash/Transaction code
description: 3 debugging challenges testing Bloom's Evaluate level: (1) B-Tree insert that doesn't check root-full before insert — show buggy pseudocode + visualization of tree growing without splits, student identifies the missing condition. (2) Hash table that resizes at 0.5 instead of 0.75 — student identifies wrong threshold. (3) App using READ COMMITTED for a bank transfer — student identifies the lost update anomaly. Each shows buggy code + wrong behavior, student finds the bug.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 debugging challenges accessible from a 'Challenges' tab or section
Each challenge: buggy pseudocode on left, broken visualization on right
Student identifies the bug by clicking the wrong line or selecting from options
Correct: 'You found it! Line 5 should check if root is full before inserting.'
Wrong: 'Look at the tree — it keeps growing but never splits. What condition is missing?'
Hint system: 3 progressive hints per challenge

title: Add scenario-based interview prep questions for database design
description: 5-10 real-world scenarios that test Bloom's Evaluate and Create: (1) 'Design the schema for Uber rides' (2) 'Your query takes 5s — EXPLAIN shows SeqScan on 10M rows — fix it' (3) 'Social media feed: normalize or denormalize?' (4) 'Payment + shipping must both succeed or both fail — what pattern?' (5) 'Choose: PostgreSQL or Cassandra for a write-heavy IoT system'. Each has guided answer framework, reference solution, and comparison with user's answer.
acceptanceCriteria: Interview Prep section with 5+ scenario questions
Each scenario: problem statement, guided answer framework ('Consider: data model, indexes, consistency'), reference solution
User can write their answer (text area) before seeing reference
Reference solution explains trade-offs, not just 'the right answer'
Framing: 'Asked at Google/Amazon/Meta' per scenario

title: Add query plan prediction: 'will this query use an index?'
description: Before generating the query plan, ask: 'SELECT * FROM orders WHERE user_id=42. Index on user_id exists. What plan will the optimizer choose?' → SeqScan / IndexScan / HashJoin. Then reveal the actual plan. This is THE most practical prediction for working engineers — it's exactly what they need to do when writing SQL. Uses existing QueryPlanSimulation with added prediction layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Prediction prompt before query plan generation in QueryPlanSimulation
User predicts the root plan node type: SeqScan, IndexScan, HashJoin, Sort, Aggregate
After prediction: plan reveals with 'Correct! The optimizer chose IndexScan because user_id has an index.'
Wrong: 'The optimizer chose SeqScan because there is NO index on status. Add one and try again.'
Works for all 5 sample queries

title: Add scoring system with XP, streaks, and per-concept mastery stars
description: Duolingo-inspired scoring: +10 XP per correct prediction (+5 speed bonus if <5s). Streak counter for consecutive correct. Per-concept mastery: 0-5 stars based on accuracy across difficulty levels. Dashboard: 'B-Tree ★★★☆☆ (60%), Hash ★☆☆☆☆ (20%), Transactions ★★★★☆ (80%)'. Weakness detection: 'You struggle with Hash Index — practice more.' Persisted to localStorage. Source: Duolingo case study — streaks boost engagement 60%.
acceptanceCriteria: XP counter visible when any practice mode is active
Streak counter with multiplier: 2x after 3 correct, 3x after 5
Per-concept mastery: 0-5 stars visible on each mode in sidebar
Progress dashboard accessible from module header
Weakness identification: 'Suggested practice: Hash Index (lowest mastery)'
All scores persisted to localStorage and restored on reload

title: Add 3-level hint system for all practice challenges
description: Wrong answers need GUIDANCE, not just 'incorrect'. 3-level progressive hints: (1) Rephrase the question: 'Think about how B-Trees decide which child to visit.' (2) Give a clue: 'Compare 35 against each key in the root: [20, 40].' (3) Show partial answer: '35 > 20, so it's NOT the left child. Now compare with 40.' Each hint costs points (-3 XP per hint used). Source: Brilliant.org hint system design.
acceptanceCriteria: Hint button appears after wrong answer on any prediction/challenge
3 progressive hints per question, each more specific
Hint 1: rephrase/reframe the question
Hint 2: algorithmic clue relevant to the specific step
Hint 3: partial solution showing the first reasoning step
Each hint used deducts 3 XP from the question's score

title: Add spaced repetition: resurface concepts after increasing intervals
description: Concepts the student has practiced should resurface at increasing intervals (1 day, 3 days, 7 days, 14 days) per spaced repetition research (Ebbinghaus + Leitner system). When student returns: 'Quick review: Which bucket does key alice hash to? (Last practiced 3 days ago)'. Concepts answered correctly get longer intervals. Wrong answers reset to 1-day interval. Persisted to localStorage with timestamps. Source: Anki algorithm, Duolingo spaced repetition.
acceptanceCriteria: On module load: check for concepts due for review (based on last practice + interval)
If concepts due: show 'Quick Review' prompt with 3-5 questions from due concepts
Correct: interval doubles (1→3→7→14→30 days)
Wrong: interval resets to 1 day
Review history persisted to localStorage with timestamps
Skip option available (but shown as 'Skip review — streak paused')
